target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent compaction overrides so each configured agent can choose its own compaction runtime behavior instead of inheriting every compaction decision from `agents.defaults.compaction`.

This is intended for multi-agent installations where the main assistant, channel-specific agents, local/self-hosted agents, and Codex/app-server workers have different runtime and transcript-management requirements.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related issues:

- #52751
- #69988
- #19501
- #15826
- #43834
- #6571
- #6572
- #30452
- #57901
- #58077
- #59863
- #60512
- #80784
- #82008

Related review/regression context addressed in this PR:

- strict `AgentEntrySchema` support for `agents.list[].compaction`
- defaults-only consumers for memory/notice/post-compaction behavior
- queued/direct compaction scope for legacy-session, non-main-agent runs

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, the config surface either rejected or only partially honored per-agent compaction settings. Several runtime paths still read from `agents.defaults.compaction` even when a specific `agents.list[]` entry defined its own compaction block.

That made these setups difficult or impossible to express correctly:

- main/default agent compacts with one provider/model
- channel or proxy agents compact with another provider/model
- local/self-hosted agents use a local provider alias for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit an OpenClaw compaction model override and keep their own runtime behavior
- non-main agents with legacy/non-agent session keys still need their active agent scope preserved during queued/direct compaction

## What Changed

This PR makes the per-agent compaction contract real end to end:

- extends the strict agent schema to accept `agents.list[].compaction`
- documents and labels the per-agent compaction fields
- resolves compaction provider/model/auth/profile choices through the active agent scope
- applies per-agent compaction settings in direct and queued compaction paths
- carries active `agentId` through preflight memory compaction, manual `/compact`, gateway session compaction, queued compaction, and direct compaction
- honors per-agent settings for:
  - `model`
  - `provider`
  - `authProfileId`
  - `timeoutSeconds`
  - `midTurnPrecheck`
  - `keepRecentTokens`
  - `truncateAfterCompaction`
  - `postIndexSync`
  - compaction memory-flush behavior
  - compaction follow-up/notice behavior
- keeps Codex/app-server agents able to omit a compaction model override so their native runtime path is not accidentally replaced by OpenClaw compaction selection
- adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent compaction scope in both direct and queued compaction

## Reviewer Notes

The important behavioral invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR passes `agentId` explicitly into compaction params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

The schema accepts the full per-agent compaction block, but this PR does not narrow the feature surface to schema-only support. The remaining defaults-only runtime consumers found during review were fixed so the accepted configuration is actually honored.

Scope audit result: after rechecking the PR surface, ancillary churn was removed from `src/config/model-refs.ts`, `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/VoiceWakeSettings.swift`, import ordering, oxlint-suppression namespace cleanup, and unrelated `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies` help wording. The subagent model-selection-only guard was restored in `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts` because it remains valid and protects the existing `subagents.model` contract.

The only intentionally retained non-feature test hygiene is `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts`, which clears the plugin metadata snapshot memo in teardown. This is included because removing it makes the full runtime-config suite fail order-dependently: `keeps shipped plugin install config records when index migration fails` receives no stale-plugin warning after earlier memoized plugin metadata leaks between tests. This does not change product behavior; it keeps the validation gate deterministic while this PR touches runtime config/schema surfaces.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Per-agent compaction overrides must resolve from the active agent scope instead of always inheriting `agents.defaults.compaction`, including legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys that require an explicit non-main `agentId`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on the PR branch plus a live OpenClaw gateway on `OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (50a2481)`, Node `v24.15.0`, host `Nano-01`, Discord channel binding for `#gb10`, native Codex app-server runtime authenticated through OAuth. Runtime proof logging was temporarily enabled at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/logs/gateway-debug-compaction-proof.log` with secrets redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a live source-module proof with `HEAD_SHA=7e5f7acdde9b052c5b41213485380ee9c11e8a0d NODE_COMPILE_CACHE= node --import tsx .artifacts/per-agent-compaction-proof.mjs`; then verified the deployed multi-agent config on a real gateway and triggered manual `/compact` in the real Discord `#gb10` channel.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript and redacted runtime log excerpt from the real setup:

```text

```

```json
  },

```

```text
2026-05-18T22:28:03.337-07:00 INFO  started codex app-server compaction sessionId=00109ec6-e729-4d21-9811-8294ad845acb
2026-05-18T22:28:31.302-07:00 INFO  completed codex app-server compaction sessionId=00109ec6-e729-4d21-9811-8294ad845acb
```

- Observed result after fix: The source-module proof selected the explicit non-main per-agent compaction override values `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `45000` ms timeout, and transcript rotation enabled for a legacy session key. The live deployed OpenClaw run accepted distinct per-agent compaction config blocks and completed a real Discord `/compact` in `#gb10`; the persisted session shows `compactionCount: 1`, `status: "done"`, and a live Codex app-server sidecar thread updated during the run.
- What was not tested: The live deployed Codex app-server compaction log does not currently print the resolved per-agent compaction model, so the live `#gb10` run is supplemental runtime proof while the source-module proof is the direct model/provider/timeout selection proof. Existing pre-restart Codex app-server sessions can also fail manual `/compact` with stale/missing native thread IDs; that appears to be a separate restart/session-recovery behavior and is not claimed fixed here.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `f6a36249b5ba495b1f33ef4db2b25fb1c8c96fa9`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=f6a36249b5ba495b1f33ef4db2b25fb1c8c96fa9 HEAD_SHORT=f6a36249b5 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (f6a36249b5, f6a36249b5ba495b1f33ef4db2b25fb1c8c96fa9)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.
